mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This mechanism is expected to be generally active across various economic conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of employment growth and equity markets
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates significantly, it signals potential future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of instances when the CPIAUCSL trigger is active, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with persistently low federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy. This environment fosters corporate revenue growth and lower borrowing costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:economic theory of demand and interest rates
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate during an economic contraction signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which typically precedes a decline in consumer confidence and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE rises significantly during a confirmed contraction, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts indicates renewed confidence in the housing market and broader economic recovery, stimulating construction activity and related industries, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by 10% month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following the HOUST trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating robust supply-side capacity and demand, which generally supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and a low unemployment rate signifies a mature and robust economic expansion. This environment supports strong corporate profits and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is high and UNRATE is low, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of rising inflation, if the Federal Reserve begins to aggressively increase the federal funds rate, it signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% month-over-month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both high inflation and rising FEDFUNDS conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.
